title: Death rate from cardiovascular diseases
subtitle: Estimated annual death rate from [cardiovascular disease](#dod:cvd) per 100,000 people.
note: To allow for comparisons between countries and over time, this metric is [age-standardized](#dod:age_standardized).
caption:
Death rate from cardiovascular diseases

Estimated annual death rate from cardiovascular disease per 100,000 people.

This choropleth world map displays the estimated annual death rate from cardiovascular disease per 100,000 people in 2023, using an age-standardized metric to allow for comparisons between countries and over time. The color scale ranges from 0 to over 500, with specific intervals marked at 100, 200, 300, 400, and 500. The darkest purple shades, indicating rates above 400 and extending beyond 500, are concentrated in a specific cluster of countries including Egypt, Sudan, Afghanistan, and Turkmenistan. Surrounding these high-rate nations, countries in North Africa, the Middle East, and Central Asia generally appear in mid-range pink shades that fall between the 200 and 400 thresholds. Russia and parts of Eastern Europe also display these darker pink tones. In contrast, the Americas show a predominance of lighter shades; the United States, Canada, and most of South America sit in the pale pink bands corresponding to values between 100 and 200. Western Europe, Australia, and Japan appear in the lightest visible shades on the map, situated near the lower end of the scale. Greenland is marked with a hatched pattern, signifying no data availability. The visual distribution highlights a distinct geographic variation, with the highest recorded death rates clustered in North Africa and Central Asia, while the lowest rates are observed across much of the Western Hemisphere and parts of East Asia.
